refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of data acquisition and processing separately and ensure their sum meets the p95 ≤ 1.0 s target under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Data acquisition and processing are sequential but modular functions
Interfaces between telemetry and processing modules are well-defined
coupling_check.notes: The chosen DPs map cleanly to the sub-FRs with minimal coupling; processing depends on acquisition but can be independently optimized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry link can sustain required bandwidth and latency under operational conditions
Processing algorithms can be optimized without compromising accuracy
Risk of network congestion impacting telemetry latency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry performance and processing efficiency during integration testing to validate assumptions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between vehicles, teleoperation, and central processing

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Limited containment as vehicle mainly provides raw data; processing is external.
Primarily consumes processed data; minimal role in update latency.
Focused on safety alerts, not involved in worksite model update.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and latency risks.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Integration complexity with vehicle telemetry and network link interfaces
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular sub-partitioning within NGHE Solution if latency bottlenecks emerge.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into acquisition and processing sub-functions; selected DPs logically address these with minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution
Interfaces are well-defined and limited to known telemetry and network links
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for these DPs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end and at interfaces using telemetry and network link monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of sub-functions to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor telemetry and network link performance during integration
Prepare contingency plans for modularizing NGHE Solution if latency targets are not met

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revisions needed; proceed to next decomposition level for detailed design.

assumptions: Telemetry and network links meet performance requirements
Processing algorithms can be optimized for latency
open_risks: Potential processing bottlenecks in NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry and processing during integration testing
